summary: British-born Mexican Surrealist painter and author. Most commonly identified by Self-Portrait (Inn of the Dawn Horse) with its hyena and rocking horse, The Giantess (The Guardian of the Egg), and Portrait of Max Ernst. Also a novelist: The Hearing Trumpet, Down Below. Friends with Remedios Varo.

works.name: Self-Portrait (Inn of the Dawn Horse) (1937–1938)
works.description: Shows Carrington sitting in a blue chair reaching toward a lactating hyena, with a rocking horse on the wall. Through the window, a white horse (symbolizing her) gallops freely.

clues.clue: Artist sits in a blue chair near a hyena and a rocking horse; white horse gallops through window (symbolizing her freedom)
examples: this artist painted a self-portrait of her sitting in a blue chair near a hyena
sits in a chair in front of a hyena and a rocking horse. Outside the house, a white horse symbolizing her can be seen
a triple-breasted hyena appears in a self-portrait...that features a rocking horse inside and a white horse outside

works.name: The Giantess (The Guardian of the Egg)
works.description: Tempera painting of a massive golden-haired woman with wheat-like hair, geese flying around/out of her white cape. Egg imagery connects to Carrington's symbolism.

images.caption: The Giantess (The Guardian of the Egg)

clues.clue: Massive golden-haired giantess with geese flying from her white cape; egg symbolism
examples: a figure with a "moonlike face," golden wheat-like hair, and geese flying out of her white cape
painted birds flying out of the white cloak of a huge golden-haired woman

clues.clue: Painted in tempera (egg tempera); used eggs to depict eggs
examples: This artist used eggs to depict eggs in her tempera paintings The Giantess

clues.clue: Three small women may symbolize Carrington, Varo, and Horna
examples: Three small women may symbolize Carrington, Varo, and Horna in Carrington's painting of a giantess "guarding" this object

works.name: Portrait of Max Ernst
works.description: Depicts Ernst in a snowy/frozen landscape wearing a red furry coat with a mermaid tail; a white horse stands behind him.

clues.clue: Man in a red coat with a mermaid tail stands in front of a frozen landscape with a white horse
examples: A man wearing a red coat with a mermaid tail stands in front of a white horse in one work by this artist, which is set in a frozen landscape
a romantic partner in a red woolen coat with a mermaid's tail and a striped sock

works.name: How Doth the Little Crocodile
works.description: Both a painting and bronze sculpture inspired by Lewis Carroll poem. Crocodiles rowing a boat, swimming through galaxies.

clues.clue: Both a painting and bronze sculpture inspired by Lewis Carroll; crocodiles rowing a boat through galaxies
examples: a sculpture and a painting by this artist inspired by Lewis Carroll depict a group of crocodiles rowing a boat
showed crocodiles swimming through a background of galaxies

works.name: The Hearing Trumpet (novel)
works.description: Surrealist novel in which Marian Leatherby uses the title device to eavesdrop on family plans to send her to a retirement home.

clues.clue: Novel: Marian Leatherby eavesdrops on family's plan to send her to retirement home using the title device
examples: wrote a novel in which Marian Leatherby eavesdrops on her family's plans to send her to a retirement home by using the title device

recursive_suggestions: Remedios Varo — close friend and fellow surrealist in Mexico
comprehensive_summary:
Leonora Carrington was a British-born Surrealist painter and author who did most of her work in Mexico. Her self-portrait Inn of the Dawn Horse (1937–1938) shows her sitting in a blue chair reaching toward a lactating hyena, with a rocking horse on the wall and a white horse galloping through the window symbolizing her freedom. The Giantess (The Guardian of the Egg) is a tempera painting of a massive golden-haired woman with geese flying from her white cape — three small women in it may symbolize Carrington, Remedios Varo, and Kati Horna.

Her Portrait of Max Ernst depicts her lover in a frozen landscape wearing a red coat with a mermaid tail beside a white horse. How Doth the Little Crocodile, inspired by Lewis Carroll, exists as both a painting and bronze sculpture showing crocodiles in galaxies. The Magical World of the Maya is a mural for the National Museum of Anthropology.

Carrington suffered a mental breakdown in Spain after Ernst's arrest and was institutionalized, experiences she documented in the memoir Down Below. She wrote The Hearing Trumpet, a surrealist novel about Marian Leatherby using the title device to eavesdrop. She grew up at the Gothic mansion Crookhey Hall, calling her father 'Lord Candlestick.' In Mexico City, she was close friends with Remedios Varo and Kati Horna ('three witches'). She illustrated Elena Poniatowska's Lilus Kikus. The 2022 Venice Biennale's main exhibition 'The Milk of Dreams' honored her.
